front: Film: El Hadji is cursed with impotence (xala) on his third wedding night; he believes it's a curse for cheating a beggar; ends with beggars spitting on the naked El Hadji
Film: El Hadji flaunts his wealth by having his Mercedes washed with imported mineral water; a rival sports a cowboy hat
Novel: Ibrahim Bakayoko leads a strike of workers on the Dakar-Niger Railway; colonial authorities turn off public water fountains; women march from Thiès to Dakar at the climax
back: God's Bits of Wood / Les Bouts de bois de Dieu (Ousmane Sembène)
work: God's Bits of Wood (Les Bouts de bois de Dieu, 1960)

front: Film: Diouana, a young Senegalese maid, is exploited by a French couple and kills herself; in the final scene, a boy returns the traditional mask she had given them
back: Black Girl / La Noire de... (Ousmane Sembène)

front: Film: Sembène's final film; Colle uses moolaade (a protective magic spell) to help women avoid female genital mutilation
Novel: a laborer is put on trial for murder after Ginette publishes his award-winning novel about the slave ship Sirius under her own name
Director: Senegalese; called the 'father of African cinema'; frequently adapted his own novels to film; trained at Gorky Film Studios under mentor Mark Donskoy
Director: proclaimed 'Why be a sunflower and turn toward the sun? I myself am the sun' when asked if his films were understood in Europe
Director: several works restored by Martin Scorsese's World Cinema Project; won the Prix Jean Vigo for Black Girl
